subject: Product Management
title: What Does a PM Actually Do?
description: A fast breakdown of What Does a PM Actually Do? for builders.

hook.text: Ask ten product managers what they do and you'll get ten different answers. That's actually the point.

buildup.text: PMs don't code, don't design, and don't sell. They decide what gets built, why, and in what order.

discovery.text: The core job is connecting three things: what users need, what the business wants, and what tech can deliver.

twist.text: PMs have enormous responsibility but zero direct authority. They can't tell anyone what to do.

climax.text: The best PMs lead through context, not control — earning trust by being the most informed person in the room.

punchline.text: A PM's power comes from clarity, not authority.

quiz.question: What is the core job of a product manager?
options: Deciding what gets built by connecting user needs, business goals, and technology
Writing code for the development team
Managing the project timeline and budget
